  "following_figure_names": "Walk (LF), Progressive Side Step, Progressive Link, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside (when preceding figure ended DC), Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line (when preceding figure ended DC), Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn (when preceding figure ended DC), Reverse Outside Swivel (when preceding figure ended DC; or normal), Basic Reverse Turn (when preceding figure ended DC), Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot (when preceding figure ended DC; or normal), Four Step, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Back Corte (step 1 in CBMP with Lady OP using CBM on 1 and 2), LF Rock (LF Rock in CBMP with Lady OP, no turn or up to 1/4 to R), Outside Swivel, Closed Promenade (having placed LF to side without weight, leading Lady to to PP, count &), Open Promenade (having placed LF to side without weight, leading Lady to to PP, count &), Natural Promenade Turn (having placed LF to side without weight, leading Lady to to PP, count &), Promenade Link (having placed LF to side without weight, leading Lady to to PP, count &), Back Open Promenade (having placed LF to side without weight, leading Lady to to PP, count &), Fallaway Promenade (having placed LF to side without weight, leading Lady to to PP, count &)",
  "notes": "Open Finish (as opposed the Closed Finish in Closed Promenade.)\nThis is not \"back and across\", LF is fairly straight on the line with RF.\nAllow shoulder and arm and upper body to rotate to right. Left shoulder is quite forward, body is twisted.\nKeep hip up/fwd toward Man (do not sit). Extend back, head left and extend. Stay very left of Man.\nDo not lower L heel too quickly till you see the center is still moving toward same direction (ie. it will be backward step); it could be a swivel, then you change moving direction.\nNote:\nMan may turn square to the lady in step 3, to end backing DC. Footwork for man would then be IE BH and for the lady IE of foot.",
